# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.

Write secure code.
# Category: kong_no_auth


apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: KongAPI
metadata: 
 name: kong-platform   # Name of the api gateway instance in Kubernetes cluster, it should be unique within a namespace and match with your ingress rules (e.g., myapp) if you are using Ingress resources or similar constructs for service discovery/ingestion into Kong API Gateway
spec: 
 servers:   # List of upstream server endpoints to route requests through when no other matches found in the routes list, can be multiple with different protocols (http and https), ports etc. defined here are http://my-service1:80 for service one & my_upstream2/api/* as an example
 - hostname: "*"  # This is a wildcard that means it will match any domain name or IP, so you can use this to route requests from all over the internet. If your services are not publicly accessible then set them up here and define routes for each service in Kong API Gateway configuration file accordingly
   port: 80    # Port number on which server is listening (http) - default value should be provided by platform if it's a microservice or application, otherwise you can use any available open ports. For example Kubernetes services usually expose their pod as service and they are exposed via the endpoints field of that object
   protocol: http  # Protocol to listen on (http) - default value should be provided by platform if it's a microservice or application, otherwise you can use any available protocols. For example Kubernetes services usually expose their pod as service and they are exposed via the endpoints field of that object
   upstream:  # List of targets to route requests through when no other matches found in routes list (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     url: "*"  # URL of the upstream server endpoint to route requests through when no other matches found in routes list (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     timeout: 50ms   # Timeout to use when connecting or reading from the upstream server (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     retries: 3   # Number of times to retry a request before failing if it fails (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     weighted_targets: []   # List of targets to route requests through when no other matches found in routes list (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     prefix: "/"   # Prefix to add before the path of upstream server endpoint in requests (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     strip_path: true   # Strip prefix from the path of upstream server endpoint in requests (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     cache_timeout: 5m   # Cache timeout to use when connecting or reading from the upstream server (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     cors: true   # Enable CORS configuration if any request is coming from a client that requires it (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     upstream_response_buffering: 5m   # Enable response buffering in the UpStream (e.g., my_upstream2) - multiple with different ports, protocols etc defined here can be http://my-service1/api/* for service one & another microservices endpoints as an example
     upstream_response_buffering: 5m   # Enable response buffering in the UpStream (e.g., my
